The focus is on providing a high level of service and becoming a trusted adviser to your clients over the long term. It's an exciting time to join the business. Recent investment has seen the company move into new offices, supported by the backing of a larger UK operation. You'll also benefit from experienced leadership, ongoing coaching and mentoring, and genuine support for further qualifications and professional development. **Key Responsibilities** \* Meet with clients to understand their financial goals, personal circumstances and future plans. \* Provide tailored advice across pensions, investments, protection and retirement planning. \* Conduct detailed financial reviews and develop appropriate recommendations. \* Prepare comprehensive client reports and suitability documentation. \* Deliver a professional and personalised client experience throughout the advice process. \* Maintain strong relationships with existing clients while helping to grow the client base. \* Ensure all work is completed in line with regulatory and compliance requirements. \* Support colleagues across the business through knowledge sharing, coaching and collaboration. **Is this you?** \* Qualified Financial Adviser with experience providing advice on pensions, investments and protection products. \* Strong understanding of financial planning principles, taxation and retirement solutions. \* Excellent communication skills with a client\-focused and professional approach. **What's on offer** \* Salary of €45,000 to €55,000\. \* Competitive uncapped commission structure. \* Hybrid working model. \* Comprehensive benefits package. \* Support towards professional qualifications and further studies. \* Ongoing coaching and mentoring from an experienced leadership team. \* Opportunity to join a business focused on quality advice rather than aggressive sales targets. \* Modern office environment and clear growth opportunities as the business continues to expand. **How to apply** For a confidential discussion about this opportunity, contact Michael Gorman or apply with your CV below. "Engage People is a multi\-award\-winning recruitment agency. We specialise across Accountancy, Financial Services, Technology, and Real Estate. To learn more about how we help create winning partnerships between professionals and organisations, get in touch today."

title: Financial Advisor
description: **Experienced Financial Adviser \- Mullingar** Full Time **Permanent** position Hybrid Role \- Mullingar Area **What we offer** We have embraced a hybrid working model for most of our positions, which means that you can enjoy a balanced approach of working from home for part of the week and working from the office for the remainder of the week. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salaries and bonuses, robust Learning and Development support, excellent Defined Contribution pension and comprehensive Wellbeing initiatives and support to name but a few. Further details on our benefits package can be accessed here Benefits (life\-careers.com) **Company Values** At Irish Life, our purpose is to help people build better futures. Our company values shape everything we do and underpin our vision of being the driving force behind Ireland’s financial, physical, and mental well\-being. We believe in **doing the right thing** —acting with integrity and making fair, responsible decisions. We **aim high** , setting ambitious goals and **find better ways** to innovate and improve. Our **customers are at the heart of everything we do** and we are dedicated to understanding their needs and exceeding their expectations. Achieving this is only possible when **we work best together** —in a supportive, collaborative environment where every idea and contribution is valued. **Role Overview** Our Financial Planning distribution which encompasses our Financial Advice Team, our Bancassurance business, our Worksite proposition and our Digital Advice team is currently embarking on an ambitious growth agenda to match changing customer attitudes and expectations around the provision of financial advice. To support this growth, we are looking for financial advisers to provide professional financial advice to individual customers and businesses on their financial needs. We are specifically seeking candidate who is based in the Mullingar area. The role operates either a hybrid working model through a blend of face to face and remote advice tailored to the customer’s needs. We also have in\-branch adviser roles in Bancassurance nationwide. The provision of financial advice is supported through the Irish Life financial advice process, with adherence to Irish Life compliance standards. **Key Accountabilities** * Develop client relationships with new and existing customers across multiple sources of business. * Provide professional best advice in developing clear financial plans for new and existing clients while delivering superior customer satisfaction. * Establish and develop relationships with small and medium sized companies to offer financial advice within the company. * Conduct professional financial reviews with customers in person and over Microsoft Teams and recommend products in adherence to Irish Life compliance standards. * Work to agreed targets and standards. * Ensure compliance with Company policies, regulatory, professional and legal requirements * Proactively protect, maintain and develop existing Client Bank * Communicate complex financial matters to customers in layman terms. * Adhere to appropriate risk management procedures to control, monitor and report on business activities. * Keep up to date with all changes in the commercial environment and completes Continual Professional Development (CPD) on an on\-going basis. **Minimum Requirements:** * Fully qualified QFA as a minimum. * Relevant 3rd Level Qualification would be an advantage. * Holding or on the path to the Retirement Planning Advisor designation would be an advantage. * As this role requires travel between locations applicants must hold a full, valid driver’s licence. **Desired Knowledge/Experience/Skills** *The successful candidate should ideally possess the following skills and knowledge:* * Significant experience providing financial advice to individuals and businesses with a proven track record of sales. * Experience in a customer facing role and be commercially aware. * Detailed knowledge and understanding of regulatory and compliance requirements of an Irish insurance company and the Central Bank. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with new and existing customers. * Possess QFA (have passed all ‘Qualified Financial Advisor’ exams) and be up to date on all CPD requirements. * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * Competent in providing financial advice on retail products * Ability to present a strong professional image of Irish Life **Fitness \& Probity** This role is a ‘controlled function’ as defined by the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 Regulations 2011\. Any appointment will be conditional on the company being satisfied that the appointee meets the requirements as set out in the Fitness and Probity standards issued by the Central Bank. This requires the company to complete prescribed due diligence to assess the appointee’s fitness and probity. Further details on this due diligence process are available from ILFS Human Resources. **About us** Irish Life is one of Ireland’s largest financial institutions with a presence across a range of financial services markets. We are one of Ireland’s leading financial services groups with over 1\.4 million customers. For over 80 years, we’ve been helping people to look after their life insurance, pension and investment needs, and more recently their health insurance. It is a subsidiary of Great\-West Lifeco and a member of the Power Financial Corporation group of companies. We are proud of our culture of developing and supporting the growth and professional development of our people. The focus has resulted in a team of top talent with high standards, positioning us at the forefront of our industry. The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Irish Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Irish Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Irish Life is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact lifecareers@irishlife.ie and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. **Irish Life Financial Services** supports Equal Opportunity. Irish Life Assurance plc is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.

title: Foreman - Structures
description: As a **Foreman \- Structures** **,** you’ll be at the heart of an exciting new West Apron Vehicle Underpass project at **Dublin Airport** . Join our dynamic team and make a difference! The **Foreman \- Structures** will be responsible for the day‑to‑day supervision and coordination of structural construction activities on a live project at Dublin Airport. Reporting to the Site Agent – Structures, the role ensures that all structural works are carried out safely, to the required quality standards, and in accordance with programme and airport operational constraints, while leading site crews and subcontractors. **About Us:** At Sacyr UK and Ireland, we’re passionate about creating a better world through our projects. As a **Foreman \- Structures** , you’ll play a crucial role in our team. Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and designed for everyone to thrive. We don’t just say it – we live it, we’re proud to have been named a **Sunday Times Top 100 Employer in both 2024 and 2025** , and to be accredited as a **Living Wage** and **Disability Confiden** t employer. **About you:** If your values align with ours, and you are passionate about being part of our team, read on! Our team is professional, great communicators, adaptable, full of integrity and generally a fabulous bunch. If this sounds like you, and you can demonstrate the required skills and experience, we want to hear from you. **Our benefits:** * 34 days’ annual leave * Employee Assistance Programme * Life assurance – 4x annual salary * Pension scheme (details above) * Cycle to Work scheme * Eye care vouchers – eye tests and glasses where required, including safety eyewear * Annual health checks with Randox Health, including half\-yearly check\-ins * Enhanced annual leave * Company\-funded language lessons – Spanish/English * Company\-funded training: training, certifications, and memberships in accredited bodies related to the position * Lighthouse Club membership * Discount shopping vouchers * Opportunity for additional professional development * And more benefits to be implemented **Key Responsibilities include:** **Supervision \& Site Management** * Supervise site crews and subcontractors carrying out structural works, including reinforced concrete, formwork, steel fixing, concrete pours, and associated activities * Plan and organise daily works in line with the short‑term programme and site priorities * Allocate labour, plant, and materials efficiently to meet programme targets * Ensure works are coordinated with adjacent trades and disciplines **Health, Safety \& Environment** * Enforce compliance with all HSEQ requirements, site rules, and airport safety procedures * Ensure method statements and risk assessments are fully understood and followed on site * Conduct daily briefings, toolbox talks, and safety inspections * Maintain high standards of housekeeping in structural work areas * Ensure works comply with airside safety rules, permits, and access requirements where applicable **Quality Control** * Ensure structural works are completed in accordance with drawings, specifications, ITPs, and quality standards * Carry out first‑line quality checks prior to engineer and client inspections * Support concrete inspections, pre‑pour checks, and snag management * Ensure defects and non‑conformances are addressed promptly **Programme \& Productivity** * Drive productivity and monitor progress against the programme * Highlight delays, constraints, and resource issues to the Site Agent – Structures * Support recovery planning and resequencing of works when required * Maintain accurate daily records of works completed and resources used **Coordination \& Communication** * Work closely with the Site Agent – Structures and Construction Engineers to deliver structural works * Liaise with subcontractors, suppliers, plant operators, and logistics teams * Communicate upcoming works, constraints, and site requirements to crews * Participate in coordination meetings and planning sessions as required **Documentation \& Reporting** * Maintain daily site diaries, progress reports, and allocation records * Assist with permits, inspections, and sign‑off documentation * Support handover activities and snag close‑out **Essential criteria:** * Legal right to work in the Republic of Ireland * Willingness to complete a 5\-year background screening as part of the hiring process * Trade background with significant experience as a Structures Foreman or Chargehand * Proven experience working on reinforced concrete and structural works * Experience supervising direct labour and subcontractors * Valid Safe Pass and ability to obtain airport security clearance * CSCS Supervisor / Safety Supervisor or equivalent * Strong knowledge of structural construction methods * Proven ability to supervise crews in safety‑critical environments * Good understanding of drawings, method statements, and construction sequencing * Strong leadership and communication skills * High level of safety awareness and attention to detail **Desirable criteria:** * Experience on airport, aviation, or other high‑security live environment * Experience on large‑scale infrastructure or civil engineering projects In return, you will be working on a high profile 'once in a lifetime' project where you will be supported by a collaborative team in a learning environment. We offer local and global career opportunities and whilst this project is based in Dublin our teams are truly global! We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We encourage applications from all backgrounds and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We are open to making reasonable adjustments to enable all applicants to participate in the recruitment process. Please let us know if you need any adjustments as part of the application process. Ready to be part of our transformative journey? Apply now and contribute to building a better future! **\#SacyrOnCourse**

title: GCC Analyst II
description: **Job Description** The GCC Analyst acts as a coordination and escalation point, working closely with internal technical teams and external customers. You’ll operate in a structured, shift‑based environment, supporting service reliability and customer experience. Focusing on techincal queries and customer support, you will play a key part in our GCC team. **Responsibilities** * Monitor data centre infrastructure and customer systems during assigned shifts * Triage alerts, incidents, and service requests in line with defined procedures * Coordinate incident response by engaging the appropriate technical teams * Track incidents through to resolution, ensuring clear and timely communication * Manage customer requests and escalations in line with SLAs * Maintain accurate documentation, logs, and shift handovers **Qualifications** * 2\+ years experience in a helpdesk, service desk, NOC, or customer service environment * Experience working in an international Business to Business environment (ideally IT, data centers, or telecommunications) is highly desirable * Comfortable working in an operational, process‑driven support role * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Good analytical and problem‑assessment skills * High attention to detail and strong organisational abilities * Focus on service quality, customer experience, and meeting SLAs * Flexibility to work 12‑hour shift patterns * Ability to remain calm, structured, and professional under pressure * Previous experience/exposure to Data Centers is highly desirable but not essential

title: Graduate Management Trainee - Dublin
description: **Overview** We’re Enterprise Mobility. A family\-owned, global mobility leader with a $39 billion turnover, nearly 90,000 team members, and operations in 95 countries. Led by CEO Chrissy Taylor, the third generation of the Taylor family, we’re built on a legacy that gives us the stability to focus on the long\-term success of our people, our customers and our business. **Why join the Graduate Management Training Programme?** As a Graduate Management Trainee, you'll have the freedom and support to explore your leadership potential \- and the opportunity to become a branch manager in one of our retail operations in as little as two years. In fact, most of our senior leaders began their careers in this very role \- including our current CEO. From day one, we’ll invest in you. You’ll be in a supportive environment where you’ll take on real responsibilities and gain invaluable hands\-on experience in customer service, sales, marketing, finance, operations, and more. **We work hard and reward hard work** Your work will be recognised with performance\-based incentives and opportunities for continued promotion. Our unique promote\-from\-within culture means you can keep your career moving forward without having to change organisations. **Award\-winning training and development** Whether you’re building on existing strengths or developing new ones, your growth is our priority. Through classroom learning, on\-the\-job training, and mentorship, you’ll have the tools and support to take the next step – and the one after that. **Our doors are open** As a Graduate Management Trainee, your experience at Enterprise Mobility will be as unique as you because you shape who we are as much as we shape your skills, path and potential. We pride ourselves on opening our doors to a variety of voices, giving way to an experience that’s both inclusive and that inspires diversity of thought. **Responsibilities** From your very first day, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility and exposed to all areas of our business. You’ll develop skills in: **Customer Service:** deliver exceptional experiences by confidently handling reservations, resolving enquiries, and building rapport with a diverse range of customers. **Sales and Marketing:** connect with local business partners, grow your network and develop lasting relationships. **Financial Control and Profitability:** understand the financial mechanics of a successful business, including cost control and interpreting profit and loss statements. **Operations and Logistics:** learn how to manage a fleet, plan strategically, and drive performance. **Leadership and Development:** take the lead in mentoring, training, and developing your own team \- with the opportunity to manage and promote others as you grow. **Qualifications** * A Bachelor’s degree is preferred; however, professional experience can be substituted if applicable. * You must have a full manual Republic of Ireland, UK or EU driving licence, but we do make accommodations for applicants who don’t drive due to a disability. * No drug or alcohol related offence on driving record within the last five years is permitted. **Additional Information** * In accordance with current employment permit regulations, we are unable to support an employment permit application for this role, as it is not eligible under the current system. * Regardless of your socio\-economic background, university attended, subject studied, and degree attained, we will always look at how you perform against our competencies and will judge you on that alone. * Please let us know about any accommodations you may need to participate in our recruitment process. * Please limit your application to only one job posting based on where you live and/or plan to work. Applying to multiple locations will delay your application being processed. * This job posting is for applications within the following location(s): Dublin

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Irish Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: IT Server & Network Hardware Support Technician
description: **Onsite IT Server \& Network Hardware Support Technician \- Kildare, Ireland \- 12\-Month Contract** **Working Pattern** * Fully Onsite * 24x7 Support Environment * 12\-hour shifts * 4 Days On / 4 Days Off rota (including day and night shifts) **About the Role** We are seeking motivated and customer\-focused IT Server \& Network Hardware Support Technicians to provide hands\-on support for network, server, and data centre infrastructure within a high\-profile enterprise environment. This role is ideal for candidates who enjoy working in data centres, supporting critical infrastructure, and collaborating with engineering and operations teams to maintain high service standards. You will be part of a dedicated onsite technical team responsible for physical hardware support, installations, troubleshooting, asset management, and infrastructure lifecycle activities. **Key Responsibilities:** Network Infrastructure Support * Provide Layer 1 network and server hardware support. * Install, remove, and connect network and server equipment. * Perform copper, fibre, and AOC cabling activities. * Support remote L2/L3 engineering teams during troubleshooting and remediation. * Coordinate and escort vendors during maintenance, warranty, and RMA activities. * Conduct cable testing and physical fault identification. * Install, label, remediate, and remove cabling infrastructure. Data Centre \& Server Operations * Perform rack, stack, build, IMAC (Install, Move, Add, Change), and decommission activities. * Support server and storage lifecycle management. * Execute physical break/fix activities on server and storage hardware. * Assist with storage and fabric\-related tasks under engineering guidance. * Perform hardware diagnostics and component replacements. * Support operating system deployment and build activities. * Conduct physical audits, asset tracking, and inventory management. * Manage media handling and hardware disposal in accordance with policy. Infrastructure Supported * Network switches and chassis * Top of Rack (ToR) switches * Firewalls and load balancers (under engineering direction) * Wi\-Fi controllers and access points * Server hardware platforms including HPE, Dell, and Supermicro * Out\-of\-band management systems including iLO, iDRAC, and IPMI Operational Responsibilities * Manage incidents and service requests through ServiceNow, Email, and Microsoft Teams. * Perform site walkthroughs and job validation checks. * Receive, stage, and manage hardware inventory. * Support equipment logistics and secure movement within facilities. * Adhere to all operational, security, safety, and compliance procedures. **Required Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * 1\-2 years' experience in a similar IT Infrastructure Support role. * Experience supporting data centre server and network hardware. * Understanding of Windows and Linux environments. * Knowledge of network infrastructure and networking concepts. * Network Administration experience. * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Strong organisational and documentation skills. * Ability to work independently with minimal supervision. * Ability to adapt quickly and learn new technologies. * Strong team\-working and interpersonal skills. * Manual handling experience. * IT\-related certification in Computer Science, Information Systems, Electronics, Networking, or similar discipline. **Desirable** Experience with: * Windows Server * Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) * Storage platforms such as 3PAR, PowerMax, NetApp, and Object Storage * Knowledge of scripting languages such as: * PowerShell * Bash * Python * Perl * DOS scripting **Apply Now** If you have experience supporting network and server infrastructure and are looking for an exciting opportunity within a mission\-critical enterprise environment, we'd love to hear from you.

title: IT Support Engineer contract
description: Role Overview We are looking for an enthusiastic and capable **Onsite Support Engineer** to provide technical support across our customer base in Dublin. In this role, you will be the key onsite representative of the company, handling installation, maintenance, and troubleshooting of hardware and software systems. You'll work both independently and as part of our wider support team, delivering a consistently high standard of service. Key Responsibilities * Install and configure desktops, servers, and networking hardware * Perform onsite troubleshooting and equipment repairs where possible * Respond to and resolve user support requests in person and remotely * Maintain detailed records of support requests and outcomes using internal ticketing systems What We're Looking For We value engineers who are: * **Enthusiastic** : Passionate about delivering outstanding customer service * **Adaptable** : Able to work across diverse projects and customer environments * **Quick Learners** : Able to rapidly absorb new tools, systems, and processes * **People\-Oriented** : Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build rapport and communicate effectively Skills \& Experience **Essential:** * 0\-1 years of experience in an IT support role * Strong knowledge of PC, laptop, and server hardware * Practical experience with: + Windows Server 2008/2012 + Microsoft Office suite + Networking \& Active Directory + Desktop operating systems (Windows 7, 8, 10; macOS) + Apple/MAC devices, Citrix environments **Desirable:** * Industry certifications such as MCP, MCITP, A\+, CCENT * Experience in working within data center environments * Exposure to ticketing systems and ITIL frameworks **Education:** * Bachelor's or Associate Degree in a relevant IT or Engineering discipline

title: Information Technology Support Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an **Information Technology Support Analyst** to join their team on a permanent basis You will have the opportunity to become part of an established and strong team and work for a company that is a market leader in their field There is an on site presence required for this role and the office is Dublin city centre based **Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to end\-users and assist in resolving various IT issues in an effective and timely manner. * Learn to identify and resolve IT problems and issues as they arise * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues on site and via cloud services * Assist in the setup, configuration, and maintenance of end\-user devices, laptops and phones * Maintain a highly organised inventory of IT assets and resources (ITAM). * Assist in monitoring and tracking issues to prevent problems occurring a second or third time. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure escalations and tickets are registered and dealt with effectively. * Contribute to IT projects, including software deployments and hardware upgrades. * Escalation of technical issues to other team members when necessary and required **Skills and Requirements:** * Minimum of 2\-3 years of experience in a technical support role. * Experience with Active Directory, Azure AD, and managing security groups. * Ability to handle access requests and managing permissions. * Proven capabilities in troubleshooting Office 365 issues and software installations. * Proactive problem\-solving skills. * Have strong verbal and written communication skills. * Demonstrated teamwork skills, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. * Enthusiasm for technology and a desire to learn and grow in the field. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities

title: Infrastructure Engineer
description: Our client is seeking an experienced Infrastructure Engineer to join their IT Service Operations team. This hands\-on role is responsible for supporting and enhancing core infrastructure, Microsoft 365 services, network operations, and security platforms. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high\-performing IT services while contributing to infrastructure projects and continuous improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Administer and support Microsoft 365, including Exchange Online, Entra ID, and Intune. * Manage Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms, and cloud infrastructure. * Support networking and firewall technologies, including Cisco and Palo Alto solutions. * Troubleshoot complex infrastructure, network, and Microsoft 365 issues. * Deliver infrastructure projects from planning through implementation. * Coordinate with external service providers and support partners to ensure effective service delivery. * Maintain security controls through monitoring, vulnerability management, and patching. * Conduct Disaster Recovery (DR) testing and maintain technical documentation. * Contribute to ongoing service improvements and operational excellence. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Third\-level qualification in Information Technology or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience in infrastructure engineering, systems engineering, or IT operations. * Strong Microsoft 365 administration and systems engineering experience. * Good understanding of networking, firewall technologies, cloud platforms, and security best practices. * Experience working within a regulated environment would be advantageous. **Desirable Certifications** * Azure Administrator Associate (AZ\-104\) * Intune Administrator (MD\-102\) * Cisco CCNA * Palo Alto PCNSA * CompTIA Security\+ * ITIL 4 Foundation **Key Competencies** * Customer Relationship Management * Planning and Organisation * Problem Solving and Decision Making * Strong Communication and Interpersonal Skills * Collaboration and Teamwork * Drive for Results **How to Apply** To be considered for this opportunity, please submit your latest CV.

title: Investment Analyst
description: **We are partnering with an international investment platform to appoint an Analyst within its Real Estate Credit team, based in Dublin.** **This is a high\-profile opportunity to join a growing platform with a strong track record, offering exposure to complex lending transactions across Europe.** **Key Responsibilities** * Support the underwriting and financial modelling of commercial real estate debt transactions * Assist in the evaluation of new investment opportunities across EMEA * Coordinate due diligence processes, working with internal stakeholders and external advisors * Contribute to investment memoranda and committee materials * Participate in deal structuring, negotiation and execution **Candidate Profile** * 1–3 years’ experience within real estate lending, structured finance, or a related field * Strong financial modelling and analytical capability * Solid understanding of commercial real estate and credit fundamentals * Clear, concise communicator with strong written and presentation skills * Highly motivated, detail\-oriented and capable of operating in a fast\-paced environment **To discuss in confidence, contact Diarmuid Clancy via dclancy@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly via the link provided.**

title: Investment Associate

title: Investment Executive
description: **Investment Executive** **About Your New Employer** Join a leading investment manager with a diverse portfolio spanning technology and renewable energy, managing over €200 million in assets on behalf of retail, family office, and institutional investors. Work at the forefront of funding innovative companies developing technology that drives productivity, resource efficiency, and meaningful environmental impact alongside strong economic returns. **About Your New Job** * As an Investment Executive, you will support the entire investment lifecycle: deal sourcing, analysis, execution, portfolio monitoring, fundraising, and investor communications. * You will play a pivotal role in screening opportunities, conducting due diligence, preparing investment material, supporting negotiations, and completing transactions. * You will develop strong relationships within the portfolio and external ecosystem, assisting in portfolio management, governance, and value\-add solutions for investee companies. * As an Investment Executive, you will contribute to fundraising initiatives, investor relations, business development, and marketing activities that advance the firm’s market profile. **What Skills You Need** * 2–4 years’ relevant professional experience in investment/corporate finance, transaction advisory, venture capital/private equity, banking/structured finance/credit, or financial modelling roles. * Strong analytical skills and confidence in interpreting financial data and business models. * Advantageous: Familiarity with EII/Venture Capital investing, climate tech, SME funding, renewable energy, or investor communications. **What's on Offer** * Make a difference in the tech and sustainability landscape, shaping the future of early\-stage companies. * Work closely with a high\-caliber team, gain mentorship, and access clear progression and development opportunities. * Competitive salary, varied responsibilities, board and investor exposure, and a supportive learning environment. **What's Next** Apply now, or contact Aoife Lowe alowe@sigmar.ie / 01474 4643 to discuss how you could make an impact in this exciting sector! If this role isn't quite right but you’re interested in similar opportunities, we’d love to connect with talented professionals.

title: Junior Office Administrator
description: **As an Office Administrator / Customer Services Administrator your main responsibilities will include, but not necessarily be limited to, the following:** * Ensuring that our customers come first is a key responsibility of the role * Dealing with clients/members and brokers/employers and other stakeholders over the phone * Processing a number of client/member requests through the new business stage / life cycle of their policies. This will involve ensuring that requests are processed in accordance with product rules and the company's processes and procedures * Complaints handling * Interacting with other areas of the business e.g. Sales, Propositions, Compliance etc. to improve how we work **Experience** * As a Customer Services Administrator your skills and qualifications will include * Has excellent inter\-personal and communication skills, particularly on the phone * Ability to work in a dynamic team environment * Be well organized, results driven and capable of working to tight deadlines * Good working knowledge of Outlook, Microsoft Word, Excel * Previous experience in a customer facing role and advantage

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Due to continued growth, new contracts and an extensive pipeline of work, Ireland’s largest Civil Contractor have an exciting opportunity for **Junior** **Quantity Surveyors** to join their team working on a large\-scale civil infrastructure projects in **Dublin.** Candidates will be working on residential and large civil infrastructure projects. **Role:** These include but are not limited to: * Procurement, appointment and management of subcontractors \& suppliers through to final account * Produce accurate progress accounts * Preparation and assessment of interim and final valuations for progress payments * Monitor and report on project costs * Collaborate with project teams to identify and mitigate potential risks and cost overruns * Assist the pre\-construction team in the delivery of tenders * Stay updated on industry innovations, regulations etc **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in Quantity Surveying. * 1 year experience in quantity surveying within the Civil Engineering sector or college placement/ internship * Strong knowledge of construction contracts, cost control, and procurement processes. * Proven experience with NEC contracts is essential. * Proficient in the use of COINS, Primavera P6, MS Project software and MS Office suite. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and negotiation abilities. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. **Salary:** * €40K – €50K neg DOE * Company Vehicle/Vehicle Allowance * Benefits Package * Pension If you are a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** considering a career move, please feel to send your CV to **N.williams@gpcireland.com** or contact **Nathan Williams** on **0861502137** for a confidential discussion. GPC will not send any applicants CVs to clients without a full discussion and prior consent. GPC is a specialist construction recruitment consultancy based in Dublin and has more than 24 years’ experience working in the recruitment of construction professionals within Ireland and Internationally.

title: Labourer
description: **Requirements:** * Previous construction site experience preferred * Safe Pass required * Manual Handling certification an advantage * Good work ethic and reliability * Ability to work as part of a team * Own transport is beneficial but not essential **What We Offer:** * Full\-time, ongoing work * Competitive rates of pay * Immediate start available * Opportunity to progress within the company If you are motivated, dependable, and looking for steady work with an established construction company, we'd like to hear from you. **To Apply:** Please email your CV Pay: €18\.00\-€22\.00 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Sick pay Experience: * Construction: 1 year (preferred) Language: * English (preferred) Location: * South Dublin, County Dublin (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Lawyer - Corporate
description: **Role: Senior Associate / Associate** **Department: Corporate** **Duration: Full time – Permanent** **The Role:** The Corporate team at William Fry consistently have a central role in some of the most sophisticated and complex corporate transactions and advisory work in Ireland. We work on large cross\-border deals with an Irish aspect. Our clients include leading Irish and international companies and institutions, public and private, as well as state and public sector bodies.We are also proud to have acted for many of Ireland’s most successful entrepreneurs. We are currently seeking to hire an experienced Lawyer to join our team. **The Person** * You have a minimum of three years' experience gained in a large. medium or boutique firm. * You have excellent technical and drafting skills coupled with a good eye for detail. * You have a track record of excellence in client service. * You are commercially minded. * You have a proven ability to manage multiple priorities and consistently work to deadline. * You have a strong work ethic and flexible approach to work **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, San Francisco, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 460 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry **i** s an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. We'll do everything we can to support you. All information disclosed will be kept confidential.

title: Listings and Fund Registration Advisor
description: **About Us** Dillon Eustace LLP is one of Ireland’s leading commercial law firms. We advise our clients on Irish and Cayman Islands law from our offices in Dublin, Cayman Islands and New York. We practice in nine principal areas: Asset Management and Investment Funds, Banking \& Capital Markets, Corporate and M\&A, Employment, Financial Regulation, Litigation and Dispute Resolution, Real Estate, Restructuring and Insolvency and Taxation. Our dynamic team of lawyers represent international and domestic clients including real estate developers and investors, asset managers, private equity firms, investment fund promoters, insurers, banks, corporates, financial institutions, government, public and supranational bodies as well as newspapers, aviation and maritime industry participants. Our Listing and Fund Registration unit is a proactive team experienced in advising all types of Irish and international fund structures on listing and distribution in European and global markets. We are committed to finding the best solutions for our clients, and we develop strong ongoing working relationships with them. The team works closely with the asset management, banking and corporate teams to offer a full seamless service to our clients. Why Us? **The Role** This junior position will support the existing listing and fund registration team. No prior listing or fund registration experience is required. The successful applicant will get full training on all aspects of fund structure and operations, and listing and distribution requirements across European and global markets. The role is client facing and wide in scope offering the opportunity to gain significant expertise and experience in funds and other financial services areas. **Key Responsibilities** * Advising clients on marketing and distribution requirements across EEA and global markets. * Liaising with clients, local regulators and fund service providers to achieve our clients’ objectives and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. * Advising clients of listing requirements across multiple exchanges, including preparing applications and management of listings for both investment funds, Exchange Traded Funds and wholesale debt across global stock exchanges. * Assisting clients with initial and ongoing regulatory filings with the Central Bank of Ireland and other regulators. * Working closely with internal teams, including asset management, banking and corporate to effectively manage projects and maximise outcomes for our clients. **Requirements** The successful candidate should be able to demonstrate the following qualifications and experience: * Appropriate financial services experience or relevant third level qualification. * Positive, pro\-active, client focused approach. * Good interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively within a flexible and busy team. * Well organised with particular attention to detail. * Good problem solving, detail oriented and analytical skills. * An adaptable, flexible mindset. * Ability to manage multiple client files and work under pressure to strict deadlines. * Ability to work on your own initiative as required and as part of a team. **Benefits** * 23 days annual leave * Health Cover * Pension Scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Hybrid working * TaxSaver Scheme * Bike to Work * Social Club. *Dillon Eustace LLP is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace which values and benefits from the diversity of our staff. We are proud of our reputation, and exceptional client service is at the core of our offering. If this sounds like a firm that you would like to work with, we would love to hear from you!*

title: Maintenance Technician
description: **Facility Maintenance Technician** **About Your New Employer** * Join a rapidly expanding player in the Irish food production sector, recognized for their commitment to quality and operational excellence. * Be part of an organization with a long\-standing presence in the industry, a reputation for innovation, and a focus on high standards and employee well\-being. * Thrive in an engaging, growth\-oriented workplace where your technical and operational input truly matters! **About Your New Job** * As the Facility Maintenance Technician, you will be responsible for the smooth functioning and general upkeep of all manufacturing areas and support facilities, rapidly resolving technical issues as they arise. * You will develop procedures to make operations more efficient, emphasizing inventory controls, reducing waste, and fine\-tuning workflow processes. * Work closely with management to align maintenance priorities with changing business needs and offer strategic support when required. * Oversee and optimize spending related to facilities and equipment, always seeking opportunities for cost improvement. * Design and update plans for emergency incidents, ensuring safety standards are maintained for everyone onsite. * Take charge of regular and preventative engineering work, including troubleshooting and repairing production machinery. * Identify technical issues using PLC diagnostics, driving continuous improvements on the factory floor. **What Skills You Need** * At least 3 years working in a similar role within manufacturing, ideally in a regulated, high\-output environment. * Fully qualified Electrician (National Craft Senior Trades or similar certification required). * Background in Lean Six Sigma settings advantageous. * Excellent analytical, communication, and organizational skills. * Residency and work eligibility for full\-time roles in Ireland. * Understanding of relevant industry standards and regulations considered a plus. **What's on Offer** * Attractive compensation package that meets or exceeds industry norms. * Opportunities to rise within an evolving team and access to ongoing skills development. * A collaborative company culture committed to employee success and supportive management. **What's Next** Excited to step up in your maintenance career? Click "Apply Now" to start your application or contact us directly to learn more. Not the exact match for you? Connect with us anyway \-there are always engineering and manufacturing roles available to discuss.

title: Marketplace Content & SEO Executive
description: **Join Luzern eCommerce: Powering the World’s Most Ambitious Brands** Step into the world of limitless possibilities with Luzern eCommerce, where we’ve been redefining online retail since 2003\. We partner with global icons like On Running, Wella, Nestlé, Mattel, Panasonic, HTC, and Philips to accelerate growth, maximize margins, and deliver exceptional customer experiences across Amazon, top marketplaces, branded webstores, and social platforms. Backed by significant investment, we’re on a mission to dominate the global eCommerce space. With our award\-winning team, cutting\-edge AI\-enabled Channel Optimizer platform, and bold strategies, we’re not just keeping up with the future of retail—we’re creating it. Be part of one of the fastest\-growing tech sectors, where you’ll innovate, collaborate, and thrive in a business that values your individuality and ambition. Join Luzern eCommerce and be part of the next big chapter in retail history! **About the Role** We are looking for a creative and commercially\-minded eCommerce Content \& SEO Executive to join the team. The right candidate will have a strong instinct for compelling product copy and a deep understanding of what makes content perform across eCommerce and D2C channels. At the core of this role is SEO. You will lead our approach to content optimisation across marketplace PDPs (Amazon and other key marketplaces) and support DTC product pages, driving visibility, click\-through and conversion through both creative content and a sharp understanding of how algorithms and search behaviour are evolving. This is not a support role; we need someone who will proactively own and push our SEO content strategy forward. Tasks **What You’ll Be Doing** **SEO Strategy \& Innovation** * Lead the SEO content strategy across marketplace listings (Amazon\-first) and DTC product pages, ensuring content is continuously optimised for visibility and conversion * Stay ahead of changes to marketplace and search engine algorithms, proactively identifying opportunities and adapting our approach before competitors do * Drive SEO innovation — testing new content formats, keyword strategies, and structural approaches to PDPs to improve ranking and performance * Conduct regular keyword research and competitor analysis to inform content decisions across all channels * Monitor SEO performance using relevant tools and translate data into clear, actionable improvements * Build and maintain a working knowledge of marketplace\-specific ranking factors **SEO Copywriting** * Write high\-quality, conversion\-focused product descriptions, titles, and bullet points optimised for both search and the customer * Adapt tone and structure for different channels — marketplace listings, DTC PDPs, and campaign content — while maintaining brand consistency * Balance SEO requirements with genuine creative quality, producing copy that ranks and reads well * Develop a strong understanding of the Client’s brand, product ranges and customers to write with authority and relevance * Comfortable with testing content optimisations to understand which performs better, based on the data **Content Optismation \& Management** * Own the end\-to\-end quality of product detail pages across marketplaces and DTC, from copy and imagery through to structured data and enhanced content (A\+ Content, Brand Stores, etc.) * Conduct regular audits of all Marketplace content elements, for both existing and prosprospective clients, to build out best in class practices and develop content strategies. * Upload and update product listings, images, and copy across eCommerce platforms, ensuring accuracy and brand alignment at all times * Manage content updates across key marketplace accounts, maintaining high standards of data quality and presentation **Reporting \& Collaboration** * Track and report on content and SEO performance, clearly communicating results and recommendations to the wider team * Liaise with internal teams and clients to gather content briefs, product information, and campaign requirements * Maintain content trackers, calendars, and documentation to keep projects on track * Support general eCommerce admin tasks where required Requirements **What You Bring to the Role** **Essential** * Amazon specific knowledge on SEO and best in class practices in relation to content creation. * An understanding of agentic commerce and how content must be optimised for AI search. * Proven creative copywriting ability with a portfolio or examples of eCommerce or product content * Solid, working knowledge of SEO, not just the basics, but an understanding of how to apply it strategically in an eCommerce context * Experience optimising marketplace PDPs, ideally Amazon, with an understanding of how marketplace search differs from Google * Comfortable working with CMS platforms and eCommerce systems * Ability to analyse performance data and use it to make informed content decisions * Strong attention to detail — accuracy in product information and copy is non\-negotiable * Highly organised, able to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines independently **Desirable** * Experience using SEO tools such as Helium 10, Jungle Scout or similar * Familiarity with Amazon Seller Central or Vendor Central and enhanced content formats (A\+ Content, Brand Stores) * Experience with product information management (PIM) systems * Understanding of DTC eCommerce platforms (Shopify, Magento, or similar) * Basic image editing ability (Figma, Canva, or similar) * Experience working with clients or across multiple brands **Personal Attributes** * Creative first — you care about the quality of the words as much as the rankings they achieve * Proactive and curious about the SEO landscape — you follow industry changes and bring ideas to the table without being asked * Commercially aware — you understand that great content has to drive results, not just look good * Detail\-oriented and methodical without losing sight of the bigger picture * A strong communicator who can explain SEO decisions clearly to non\-specialists * Collaborative team player who can also work independently and manage their own time * Genuine interest in eCommerce, retail, and digital marketing Benefits **What We Offer** * **Competitive Compensation:** Attractive salary package with benefits. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Competitive annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * **Growth Opportunities** : Access to learning and development programs to enhance your skills. * **Culture and Environment** : A fun, inclusive, and collaborative workplace. * **Wellbeing Support:** Access to an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) * **Quarterly Social Events:** Company\-sponsored quarterly social events designed to bring teams together, celebrate success, and strengthen connections across the business. We are committed to equal opportunities and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Network Administrator

title: Network Operations Engineer
description: Job Description Sigma Wireless are seeking a reliable and motivated Level 1 NOC Engineer to join our Network Operations team based in Dublin. This role operates within a 24x7 environment from our Sigma Finglas office, and involves 12\-hour shifts, primarily nights, including weekends and public holidays. The role supports a mission\-critical private LTE network, along with other live customer networks and services. The successful candidate will be responsible for real\-time monitoring, first\-line incident triage, and escalation to ensure continuous network availability and compliance with service targets. This is an excellent opportunity for candidates looking to build hands\-on experience in telecoms and network operations within a live operational environment. **Primary Responsibilities** **Network Monitoring** * Monitor network infrastructure (EPC, RAN, CPE) using NOC tools and dashboards to identify faults or performance issues. * Respond to system alarms and incidents, ensuring timely acknowledgement and action. **Incident Handling** * Perform first\-line incident triage, including initial diagnosis, impact assessment, and prioritisation. * Carry out basic troubleshooting activities (e.g. connectivity checks, alarm validation, remote resets where applicable). * Escalate unresolved or complex issues to Level 2 or third\-party vendors and follow through to resolution. **Operations \& Coordination** * Log, track, and update incidents in the ticketing system, ensuring accurate and clear documentation. * Coordinate with customer support teams during incidents to ensure aligned response and communication. * Provide clear and timely updates on incident status, impact, and resolution progress. * Coordinate with field engineers and external partners to support on\-site fault resolution where required. * Work in line with incident management processes and SLA targets. * Maintain clear communication with internal teams and stakeholders during live incidents. * Participate in structured shift handovers to ensure continuity across 24x7 operations. **Qualifications \& Skills** * Relevant qualification in Telecommunications, Networking, IT, Computer Science, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). * Basic understanding of IP networking and telecom concepts. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to remain calm and organised while handling multiple incidents. * Strong attention to detail and ability to follow defined operational processes. * Effective written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. * Comfortable working 12\-hour night shifts in a 24x7 operational environment. **Desirable attributes** * Understanding of LTE / wireless networks * Certifications such as CCNA, ITIL or similar. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * Customer\-focused attitude and commitment to service excellence. **Non\-EEA nationals** must have an employment permit to work in Ireland. Please visit link below for more information Types of employment permits (citizensinformation.ie)

title: Part-time Sales Associate
description: **Love a job where every day looks a little different?** Our newest store just opened in Jervis. We're looking for someone who's great with people and even better with details. **MemoryBlock Ireland is hiring seasonal part\-time staff for our new Jervis Centre store in Dublin City.** You'll be the face of the brand in\-store: helping customers turn their favourite photos into something they'll keep for life, made right there in front of them. **What you'll be doing:** * Engaging with customers, understanding what they're looking for, and recommending the right product for the moment * Handling cash and our POS system, entering orders accurately * Keeping displays, shelves, and the store itself looking sharp and on\-brand * Juggling multiple customer requests at once and hitting order turnaround times * Comfortable with email, online order systems, and basic digital photo handling (we'll train you on our tools) * Restocking and light physical tasks \- you'll be on your feet for parts of the shift * Flexible across weekends and peak shopping periods **What we're looking for:** Retail, gifting, or customer\-facing experience is a bonus but not essential. What matters more is that you're a quick learner, comfortable talking to people, and good under pressure during busy moments. Photoshop knowledge is a nice plus. **You'll also need to be flexible to rotate across our stores when needed, including Blanchardstown, Nutgrove, and Jervis.** This is a seasonal part\-time position that has a chance of extension if you show exceptional skills and ability and if the open position presents itself. **This is a seasonal part\-time position based at our Jervis Centre store.** Think this sounds like you? Apply now and tell us why. Get in touch today \- Email us at karmika@memoryblock.ie

title: Performance Tester
description: SDET (Performance Tester) 12\- 24 months 3 days in the office Dublin 700\-720 per day ***Job Description*** *We are seeking a **Senior Software Engineer** with a strong focus on **performance** **testing** to help raise quality, reliability, and release confidence across distributed **Java\-based platforms.** You will design and evolve modern test automation approaches and frameworks, partner closely with engineers/SRE/product to shift quality left, and ensure we validate end\-to\-end customer journeys through a balanced strategy spanning functional, integration, performance, and operational (chaos\-style) testing.* *Role* ***Design, build, and maintain test automation frameworks*** *that enable fast, reliable validation across service, integration, and end\-to\-end layers (e.g., contract tests, journey tests, and regression suites).* *Implement and continuously improve automation for distributed Java platforms, including test data strategy, service virtualization/mocking, and repeatable test environments* *Integrate automated testing into CI/CD pipelines, ensuring quality gates and actionable reporting to support continuous delivery* *Lead performance testing strategy and execution using JMeter and BlazeMeter, including workload modeling, trend analysis, and performance regressions as part of release readiness.* *Contribute to operational/chaos testing to validate system behavior under adverse conditions (e.g., component failure, network degradation, resource contention) and to strengthen resilience.* *Drive effective API and protocol\-level testing, including HTTP\-based services and SFTP workflows, focusing on correctness, security posture, and reliability in distributed deployments.* *Communicate clearly and collaborate across distributed teamsâ€"translating quality risks into practical engineering actions, facilitating alignment on test strategy, and mentoring others in test automation best practices.* *All About You* *Demonstrated hands\-on expertise implementing automation at scale for distributed Java platforms (e.g., microservices), with strong understanding of test layering (unit/service/contract/integration/journey).* *Strong hands\-on experience building or extending test frameworks using common testing patterns (TDD/BDD where appropriate) and tooling; able to evaluate and apply frameworks effectively for different test needs.* *Proven ability to design automated tests that are reliable, maintainable, and fastâ€"using effective mocking/service virtualization and production\-like environment simulation where needed.* *Strong performance testing experience with JMeter and BlazeMeter, including interpreting results, diagnosing bottlenecks, and establishing performance baselines/regression thresholds.* *Experience implementing or contributing to operational/chaos testing practices to validate failure modes and improve resilience.* *Excellent written and verbal communication skillsâ€"able to explain technical issues succinctly, influence quality\-first engineering habits, and partner effectively across functions and geographies.* *Desirable / additional capabilities* *Experience with HTTP/API testing practices and tooling (e.g., service\-level automation, contract testing) and ability to apply these patterns consistently across teams.* *Experience validating SFTP\-based workflows and file\-transfer style patterns (e.g., reliability, retries, idempotency, negative\-path testing).* *Familiarity with CI/CD orchestration and quality gates (e.g., pipeline\-driven test execution, reporting, and release readiness signals).* *Strong approach to metrics and continuous improvement (e.g., defect trends, coverage effectiveness, stability of suites, and release quality signals) to guide prioritization and investment."* **Desired Skills and Experience** SDET (Performance Tester) 12\- 24 months 3 days in the office Dublin 700\-720 per day Job Description We are seeking a Senior Software Engineer with a strong focus on performance testing to help raise quality, reliability, and release confidence across distributed Java\-based platforms. You will design and evolve modern test automation approaches and frameworks, partner closely with engineers/SRE/product to shift quality left, and ensure we validate end\-to\-end customer journeys through a balanced strategy spanning functional, integration, performance, and operational (chaos\-style) testing.

title: Platform Owner - Public Cloud
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Do as a Platform Owner – Public Cloud** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Lead and scale Cloud Engineering \& Operations, partnering with strategic technology vendors to deliver secure, self‑service cloud platforms that accelerate product team delivery and customer value. * Establish robust cloud governance and standards, ensuring excellence across cost management, security operations, deployment practices, compliance, and policy adherence. * Drive operational performance and service quality, defining metrics and controls to improve availability, workload utilisation, SLA performance, service transition rigour, and security posture. * Provide hands‑on technical leadership, setting cloud strategy and architecture direction, resolving complex escalations, and communicating clearly with senior stakeholders. * Build and develop high‑performing engineering teams, including line management, mentoring, talent strategy execution, and fostering accountability through servant leadership. * Support cloud transformation and strategic alignment, ensuring roadmaps, backlogs, and operating models align with business objectives while strengthening senior stakeholder and partner relationships. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Proven cloud leadership experience (8\+ years) leading Cloud Engineering and/or SRE teams, with a strong engineering background across enterprise‑scale systems, platforms, and operational environments. * Deep expertise in Microsoft Azure, delivering secure, scalable, and fault‑tolerant architectures using PaaS, serverless, containerisation, and Infrastructure‑as‑Code (Terraform preferred), with working knowledge of GCP as an advantage. * Strong operational and financial acumen, including cloud cost management, budgeting, forecasting, workload optimisation, and governance across security operations, compliance, and policy enforcement. * Advanced DevOps, CI/CD and observability capabilities, with hands‑on experience in logging, monitoring, reliability engineering, enterprise‑grade deployments, and advocating modern Agile, Lean, and DevOps practices. * Visionary and forward‑thinking technologist, able to identify emerging technologies, translate them into business value, inspire new ways of working, and successfully lead transformation initiatives. * Exceptional people and partner leadership skills, including managing and influencing third‑party providers, mentoring high‑performing engineering communities, and excelling in stakeholder management, problem solving, and multi‑stream delivery. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-4307
