title: Store Colleague
description: Established in 1981 with a single store in the Northwest of England, the JD Group is a leading omni\-channel retailer of Sports Fashion, Outdoors and Gyms with our colleagues working in stores across several retail fascias in many markets around the world. JD Sports Fashion Plc was listed on the London Stock Exchange in 1996 and has been a FTSE100 publicly quoted company since 2019 and continues to grow in the UK and internationally. We want to be the leading global omnichannel retailer in the sports and outdoor industry. To be a part of this successful company and help us to achieve this you will have the desire to ingrain our strategic goals of being a people\-led, innovative and customer\-focused organisation which provides operational excellence whilst identifying new areas of growth as part of our day to day objectives. This is JD. Born in 1981\. Built for Now. At JD, we don’t just sell sneakers and outdoor gear, we set trends, break boundaries, and build communities. Whether you're on the shop floor, behind the scenes, or driving innovation, every role here plays a part in shaping the future of our industry. We’re fast\-paced, fearless, and unapologetically ambitious. We celebrate authenticity, thrive on collaboration, and never stop pushing forward, if you’re ready to bring your energy, ideas, and personality to a team that values them. This is your moment. This is JD. Are you in? \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Hourly Rate \- £14\.25 Role Overview At JD Group, we don’t just keep up with the game — we set the pace. As a Store Colleague, you’ll play an important role in delivering outstanding experience every day, helping customers feel valued, supported, and excited about our products. You’ll bring energy to the shop floor by greeting customers confidently, keeping displays looking sharp, replenishing stock, and maintaining high service and presentation standards. With a positive attitude, great teamwork, and a passion for helping people, you’ll help keep the store running smoothly and showcase the high standards we are known for. If you’re ready to step into a fast\-paced retail environment, learn new skills, and be part of a supportive team, this is your opportunity to make an impact and build a strong foundation for your future career. Key Responsibilities * Welcome customers with confidence and energy, offering friendly support and helping them find the products they love. * Use in\-store devices to check stock, share product options, and support the full customer experience. * Keep the shop floor clean, tidy, and fully replenished — making sure displays stay fresh and on\-brand. * Support day\-to\-day store tasks, including till work, restocking, and following all safety and store guidelines. Skills \& Experience Required * A positive, enthusiastic approach suited to a fast\-paced, customer\-focused retail environment. * Strong communication skills, with confidence engaging customers and working as part of a team. * Willingness to learn, take on new tasks, and grow your skills in a supportive store environment. * Flexibility to work various shifts, including weekends, evenings, and busy seasonal periods. Desirable Attributes * A genuine passion for JD’s brands, trends, and the latest products. * Proactive attitude, with the confidence to approach customers and support sales opportunities. * Strong attention to detail, ensuring the shop floor always looks sharp and ready. * Self\-motivation, with a desire to improve and progress within JD Group. What We Offer We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, committed to creating a workplace where every colleague is valued, respected, and empowered. We celebrate the diversity of our teams and strive to ensure that all colleagues regardless of background, identity, or lived experience can thrive and contribute meaningfully. As a part of our successful and continually evolving company, you’ll be encouraged to embrace and uphold our strategic priorities: putting people first, at the forefront of digital innovation, staying deeply attuned to our customers, and pursuing operational excellence. We are always exploring new avenues for growth, and we welcome individuals who are passionate about making a positive impact every day. We know our employees work tirelessly to make JD Sports the success it is today and in turn, we offer them some amazing benefits: * Incremental Holiday Allowance * Staff Discount on qualifying purchases across Group retail stores and online * Exclusive Colleague Bike Discount scheme * Discounted Gym membership * Personal development opportunities to learn and develop at work * Access to Apprenticeships and accredited qualifications About JD Group Founded in 1981 with a single store in the Northwest of England, JD Group has grown into a leading global omni\-channel retailer in Sports Fashion, Outdoors, and Gyms. Our diverse and dedicated teams operate across a portfolio of renowned retail brands in multiple international markets. Listed on the London Stock Exchange since 1996 and a proud member of the FTSE100 since 2019, JD Group continues to expand both in the UK and globally, driven by a commitment to innovation, excellence, and possibility. Our vision is to become the world’s most trusted and dynamic omni\-channel retailer in the sports and outdoor industry. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us in shaping this future. If you're passionate about contributing to an inclusive, people\-first, and customer\-centric organisation and are motivated by continuous growth and operational excellence, we’d love to hear from you. We know our colleagues work tirelessly to make JD Sports the success it is today and in turn, we offer them some amazing benefits including staff Discount On JD Group and other brands within the organisation and personal development opportunities to learn and develop at work. title: Tax Technology & Transformation - Associate
description: **Line of Service** Tax **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** General Tax Consulting **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** About us: PwC Ireland’s Tax Technology \& Transformation team is part of Ireland’s leading Tax practice. We are a dynamic Partner led team with diverse Tax, Finance and IT backgrounds. Our Tax Technology \& Transformation team works with market leading clients in a broad range of industry sectors to maximise operational effectiveness within their tax functions by leveraging best in class approaches and solutions. We help clients design and execute the right tax technology strategies to manage tax risks, increase efficiency and take advantage of the opportunities enabled by technology. The tax landscape is changing and we are continuously expanding due to our clients’ growing demand to leverage more efficient processes and technology to support the tax function as the volume and complexity of global tax compliance and regulatory requirements continues to increase. **What we do:** Our work is increasingly varied and involves supporting organisations with all aspects of tax technology and transformation across all tax heads including: * The development of long term tax function technology strategies and alignment of same with wider business operating model objectives; * The design, build and implementation of tax solutions solving tax problems leveraging best\-in\-class artificial intelligence, robotics, data extraction, transformation and analytics functionality; * The implementation of our market leading tax compliance and reporting solutions as well as tax workflow management and PwC hosted Software as a Service (SaaS) solutions; * Working with PwC alliance partners to optimise core systems to support the delivery of data in a fit\-for\-purpose for tax process execution. **What does this role offer?** The changing tax landscape driven by a significant shift to technology initiated challenges by tax authorities, presents an opportunity for tax functions to respond with a technology enabled response. These developments afford our team regular client interactions and the opportunity to support in understanding the tax challenges presented along with the design and implementation of solutions to help them add value in their organisations. You will work on a varied portfolio of tax projects with our key clients and develop a broad range of technology and tax consulting skill sets. **About you – Key Qualifications/Skills \& Experience:** * Part ACA / ITI qualified (or equivalent) preferable * 3 years finance or tax transformation project management experience in industry or practice * Proven experience designing and implementing tax / finance transformation automation solutions * Certification or functional experience with technology solutions such as the MS Power Platform, Python, SQL, Alteryx etc ***Note:** This role is not open for relocation or work\-permit sponsorship.* **Create your future with us.** The world of work has changed and we are changing with it! At PwC, we believe that challenges are better solved together. A career with us means joining a purpose led and diverse, global community where you will do meaningful work with our amazing clients. Our culture is unique and what sets us apart. Our people really are at the very heart of our business. So, when you join us, you will be empowered to work flexibly in a way that works for you, your clients and the teams around you, supporting your wellbeing. We work tirelessly so that you can unlock your full potential and give you everything you need to take your career where you want it to go. We appreciate what you bring to our culture,our team and our clients and we recognise and celebrate you for it. Our tech\-powered and global capabilities come together so that you can make an impact not only to clients and our teams but on the world around us too. **PwC Perks** Of course, we also offer all the perks you would expect from a leading employer for every stage of your journey with us. From everyday flexibility to working from abroad, learning and development or reward and recognition. We have best in class inclusive and family friendly perks and policies that meet you where you are\- in your life and in your career. **Inclusion Statement** PwC is proud to be an equal opportunities employer and we believe passionately in employing a diverse workforce so much that we have signed up for the Business in the Community Elevate Pledge. We recognise that confidence and the imposter syndrome can sometimes hold you back from applying for your dream job. Whatever your path is or however you identify, please don’t hesitate to apply \- we would love to hear from you! You can learn more about how we are driving a culture of belonging at PwC, and explore our range of inclusive programmes, initiatives, employee resource groups and more, by visiting www.pwc.ie We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Communication, Domestic Restructuring (Taxes), Emotional Regulation, Empathy, Inclusion, Income Tax Provisions, Intellectual Curiosity, Optimism, Partnership Tax, Retail and Consumer (Taxes), Tax Credit, Taxes, Tax Incentives, Tax Legislation, Tax Modeling, Tax Policy, Tax Provisions, Tax Restructuring, Tax Structures, Teamwork, Technical Writing, Well Being **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** Not Specified **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** Yes **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

description: **About Hydraquip** Hydraquip Ltd is a leading supplier of hydraulic, pneumatic and automation solutions to customers across Ireland. Working with globally recognised engineering brands, we provide technical expertise and solutions to industries including manufacturing, food production, agriculture, construction and engineering. We are seeking a motivated and technically minded individual to join our Technical Solutions Development Programme based in Naas. This opportunity is open to candidates from a wide range of backgrounds. While engineering qualifications are advantageous, they are not essential. Full training will be provided to the right candidate. What matters most is a strong work ethic, curiosity, a willingness to learn and the ability to take initiative. **The Role** The successful candidate will work alongside our experienced engineering team, developing technical knowledge while supporting customer enquiries, product selection and engineering solutions. Key Responsibilities • Support customers with technical enquiries and product selection • Learn hydraulic, pneumatic, electrical and automation systems • Prepare technical quotations and proposals • Work with suppliers and internal teams to deliver customer solutions • Develop relationships with customers across multiple industries • Gain exposure to automation projects and system design activities • Utilise SAP Business One and other engineering software tools • Progress towards managing customer accounts and technical projects **Training \& Development** Hydraquip is committed to investing in the development of its people. The successful candidate will receive: • Structured on\-the\-job training • Mentoring from experienced engineers • Exposure to real engineering and automation projects • Ongoing technical and commercial development • Support for further education and professional qualifications • Clear long\-term career progression opportunities **Who We Are Looking For** Essential • Excellent work ethic and self\-motivation • Genuine interest in engineering, mechanical or electrical systems • Strong communication skills • Problem\-solving ability and attention to detail • Good organisational and IT skills • Positive attitude and willingness to learn Desirable • Qualification in engineering, science or a technical discipline • Experience in a technical, engineering or customer\-facing environment • Interest in automation, hydraulics, pneumatics or electrical systems • Full driving licence **Application Process** Apply through LinkedIn Easy Apply or submit your CV to patrick@hydraquip.ie Hydraquip Ltd is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates and are committed to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process.

title: Technical Support Engineer
description: **Job Title: Technical Support Engineer** **Duration: 11Months Contract (24/7 Operations)** **Location: Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid – 3 days onsite)** **Role Overview** We are seeking a skilled **Technical Support Engineer.** This role focuses on supporting mission\-critical environments, with a strong emphasis on **database troubleshooting, performance analysis, and incident management** . The successful candidate will work in a **customer\-facing, regulated environment** , handling high\-severity incidents and collaborating closely with engineering and platform teams to ensure availability, stability, and performance. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide advanced technical support for ServiceNow platform issues with a focus on **database\-related incidents** * Investigate and resolve **P1/P2 production issues** in a high\-availability environment * Analyze database performance, identify bottlenecks, and support root cause analysis * Read, interpret, and troubleshoot scripts and platform logic * Use Linux/UNIX commands and shell environments for log analysis and troubleshooting * Communicate clearly and effectively with customers and internal stakeholders during incidents * Maintain strong ownership of issues from identification through resolution * Operate confidently in a **regulated and sensitive environment** **Required Skills \& Experience** **1️⃣ Database Expertise (Critical Requirement)** * Strong, **hands\-on production experience** with enterprise databases * Experience with one or more of the following: * MySQL * PostgreSQL * Oracle * Other enterprise RDBMS * Proven ability to: * Write and analyze SQL queries * Investigate database\-related issues * Understand and diagnose performance bottlenecks * **Academic\-only database experience will not be considered** **Nice to Have:** * Database tuning and optimization * Performance diagnostics * Experience diagnosing platform\-level performance issues * Background in **Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)** environments **Scripting / Programming Knowledge** * Strong understanding of at least one object\-oriented or scripting language: * JavaScript * Python * Other scripting languages * Ability to: * Read and interpret code * Debug and troubleshoot scripts * Understand logic flow and execution paths * Writing code from scratch is **not required** **Operating Systems \& Tools** * Comfortable working with: * Linux / UNIX environments * Shell commands and utilities * Experience using these tools for troubleshooting and log analysis **Soft Skills \& Behavioral Competencies** * Excellent communication skills ( **critical requirement** ) * Prior experience in **customer\-facing technical support** roles * Calm, mature, and composed under pressure * Strong multitasking ability during critical incidents * High sense of ownership and accountability * Comfortable working in regulated, compliance\-driven environments **Experience Benchmark** * relevant technical support experience preferred * may be considered if: * Database experience is strong and hands\-on * Candidate demonstrates maturity and enterprise exposure

title: Test Analyst
description: We are seeking an experienced IT Test Analyst to join a dynamic technology team responsible for delivering high\-quality digital solutions. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring that new systems, enhancements, and integrations meet defined business and technical standards. This is a hands\-on testing role requiring strong analytical ability, a structured approach to quality assurance, and a commitment to excellence. **Role Purpose** The IT Test Analyst will be responsible for planning, executing, and coordinating all phases of software testing across multiple projects. This includes developing test strategies, designing test cases, managing test environments, and collaborating closely with development and business teams to ensure successful delivery. **Core Responsibilities** * Analyse business and technical requirements to understand system functionality and testing needs. * Design and maintain comprehensive test plans, scripts, and scenarios to validate functionality, usability, performance, and security. * Conduct manual and automated testing activities to verify system behaviour and data integrity. * Manage test environments, datasets, and configurations to support accurate and repeatable test execution. * Identify, log, and track software defects through to resolution using appropriate tools and processes. * Provide clear, concise reporting on test outcomes, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with developers, analysts, and project managers to resolve issues and enhance solution quality. * Support the continuous improvement of testing processes, tools, and documentation standards. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a software testing or QA role within a technology\-focused environment. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Proven experience testing web, cloud, or enterprise applications. * Strong knowledge of software testing principles, tools, and methodologies. * Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and common delivery approaches such as Agile or Waterfall. * Experience with defect management and test management tools (e.g., JIRA, Azure DevOps, TestRail). * Ability to create and maintain traceability between requirements, test cases, and results. * Excellent analytical, documentation, and communication skills. * Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high\-quality work. * Ability to work independently and contribute effectively within a team setting. **Desirable Attributes** * Professional certifications in software testing or quality assurance (e.g., ISTQB Foundation Level or equivalent). * Experience with test automation frameworks or scripting languages. * Exposure to API, integration, or performance testing. * Familiarity with DevOps and CI/CD environments. * Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a proactive approach to learning new tools and technologies. **Personal Qualities** * A structured thinker who enjoys problem\-solving and continuous improvement. * Excellent communicator who can translate technical concepts for non\-technical stakeholders. * Reliable, accountable, and focused on delivering results. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities.

title: Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme - Business/Marketing
description: Our Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme (Business/Marketing Stream) will commence in October 2026\. This is a unique two\-year programme designed to provide graduates with broad exposure to many aspects of the Irish horse racing industry. As part of Horse Racing Ireland (HRI), you will gain hands\-on experience across a range of functions including marketing, communications, project coordination, and industry engagement. You will contribute to key initiatives that support the promotion, development, and sustainability of the industry. **Programme Rotations** The programme will encompass rotations in the following areas: **HRI Marketing** Graduates will begin their journey within the central HRI Marketing team, gaining exposure to national campaigns, brand development, and audience engagement. You will support the planning and execution of marketing initiatives across digital, social media, and traditional channels, helping to promote racing and drive attendance and participation. **National Equine Campus (New Development)** This rotation offers a unique opportunity to contribute to the development of a major strategic initiative for the industry. Graduates will support a range of business and operational activities within the National Equine Campus, including stakeholder engagement, programme delivery support, and project coordination. You will also assist in promoting education, training, and wellbeing programmes delivered through Equuip and RACE, while gaining exposure to the broader development and implementation of a large\-scale industry project. **Leopardstown Racecourse \- Marketing Department** At Leopardstown Racecourse, graduates will gain hands\-on experience in racecourse marketing, supporting the delivery of high\-profile racing festivals and events. You will work on campaigns that enhance customer experience, drive attendance, and promote the racecourse as a premier sporting and entertainment venue. **Equine Welfare** Graduates will support a range of activities within the Equine Welfare function, a core priority for HRI and the wider industry. This placement will provide experience across welfare administration, industry communications, and project coordination. You will contribute to supporting welfare initiatives, assisting with internal and external communications, coordinating key projects, and providing general administrative support. There will also be opportunities to assist with digital and social media content where relevant, helping to support awareness and engagement. **Programme Experience** In addition to your rotations, you will: * Contribute to a range of marketing, communications, and business initiatives supporting major race meetings and national programmes * Gain experience across marketing, content development, stakeholder engagement, and project coordination * Develop a strong understanding of how commercial, operational, and marketing functions work together to support the racing industry * Work across HRI locations, including Newbridge (Co. Kildare), KIldare and Leopardstown Key Qualities We Are Looking For * Educational Background: Completed a Level 8 honours degree (or higher) in Business, Marketing, Communications, or a related discipline within the last two years * Passion for Marketing: A strong interest in marketing, branding, and customer engagement * Interest in Racing: Enthusiasm for the horse racing industry and its continued growth * Creative and Innovative: Ability to think creatively and contribute fresh ideas * Strong Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Team Player: Ability to collaborate effectively with colleagues and stakeholders * Relevant Experience: Internships, academic projects, or extracurricular involvement demonstrating marketing or communication skills * Adaptability: Ability to work in a fast\-paced, event\-driven environment, with flexibility to work evenings and weekends when required * Transport: Full driving licence and access to transport is required **Why Join Horse Racing Ireland's Graduate Programme?** This programme offers a unique opportunity to launch your career within a nationally significant and internationally recognised industry. You will gain exposure to a diverse range of business and marketing activities, work on impactful initiatives, and contribute to the ongoing development of Irish horse racing. **Selection Process:** * Application Submission: Complete the application questions, providing details about your educational background, relevant experiences, and motivations for joining the graduate programme. * Application Review Our recruitment team will review all applications to assess qualifications, skills, and alignment with the programme's objectives. This stage involves a careful examination of academic achievements, extracurricular activities, and any additional supporting information provided. * Selection Assessments Shortlisted candidates are invited to complete a set of selection assessments designed to evaluate aptitude, analytical skills, and suitability for the specific roles within the graduate programme. * First Round Interviews Successful candidates from the assessment stage are then invited to participate in first\-round interviews. These interviews are typically conducted by the HR team and hiring managers and will focus on exploring your motivations, experiences, and suitability for the programme. * Second\-Round interviews with Senior Management Candidates who successfully navigate the initial interview stage are invited to a second round, where you will have the opportunity to interact with senior management. This stage delves deeper into candidate's potential contributions to the organization and allows senior leaders to assess the alignment of the candidate with the overall strategic goals of the company. * Final Selection After thorough evaluation and consideration of all stages, the final selection is made. Successful candidates will be offered a place on the Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme, marking the beginning of your professional journey with the organisation. HRI is an equal opportunities employer, and we are committed to inclusivity and accessibility. We will provide any necessary reasonable accommodation for candidates during the interview process.

title: Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme - Racecourse Operations
description: Our Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme (Racecourse Operations Stream) will commence in October 2026\. This is a unique two\-year programme designed to immerse you in the dynamic and multifaceted world of racecourse operations within the Irish horse racing industry. As part of Horse Racing Ireland (HRI), you will gain hands\-on experience across key operational, commercial, and racing functions, developing a deep understanding of what it takes to deliver world\-class race days and support the continued success of the sport. **Programme Rotations** The programme will encompass rotations in areas such as the following: **Racecourses** Graduates will immerse themselves in the fast\-paced world of racecourse operations and event management. You will actively contribute to the planning and delivery of both racing and non\-racing events, gaining hands\-on experience in logistics, scheduling, customer experience, and on\-the\-day coordination. This placement provides a comprehensive understanding of the operational complexities involved in hosting successful race meetings. **HRI Racecourses Commercial Department** During this rotation, graduates will gain exposure to the commercial side of the racing industry. You will support activities relating to sponsorships, partnerships, marketing initiatives, and revenue generation. This experience will help you build a strong foundation in commercial strategy while understanding how racecourses drive growth and enhance customer engagement. **Racing Department** Within the Racing Department, graduates will work closely with industry professionals to support the day\-to\-day running of racing. You will gain valuable insights into race planning, regulatory frameworks, and the governance of the sport, as well as the coordination required to ensure the smooth delivery of race meetings. **Leopardstown Racecourse Masterplan Project** Graduates will also gain exposure to strategic development projects, including the Leopardstown Masterplan. This provides a unique opportunity to understand long\-term planning, capital development, and the evolution of racecourse facilities to enhance the customer and stakeholder experience. **Programme Experience** In addition to your rotations, you will: * Attend horse sales and experience the commercial and bloodstock side of the industry * Engage in Racehorse Ownership open days, gaining exposure to owners and syndicates * Work across HRI locations, including Newbridge (Co. Kildare) and racecourses such as Fairyhouse, Navan, Leopardstown, and Tipperary * Develop a well\-rounded understanding of the operational and strategic elements that make the racing industry unique **Key Qualities We Are Looking For** * Educational Background: Have completed a Level 8 honours degree within the last two years * Passion for Racing: A genuine interest in working within the horse racing industry * Ambitious and Innovative: A drive to succeed and bring new ideas in a dynamic environment * Team Player: Strong interpersonal skills with a collaborative mindset * Relevant Competencies: Previous work experience, internships, or extracurricular activities demonstrating transferable skills * Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment, with flexibility to work evenings and weekends when required * Communication Skills: Strong organisational and communication abilities, particularly in event\-driven environments * Transport: Full driving licence and access to transport is required **Why Join Horse Racing Ireland?** This programme offers a unique opportunity to build a career at the heart of Ireland's horse racing industry. You will gain invaluable hands\-on experience across racecourse operations, commercial activities, and racing governance, working alongside experienced professionals and contributing to the delivery of high\-profile racing events. **Selection Process:** * Application Submission: Complete the application questions, providing details about your educational background, relevant experiences, and motivations for joining the graduate programme. * Application Review Our recruitment team will review all applications to assess qualifications, skills, and alignment with the programme's objectives. This stage involves a careful examination of academic achievements, extracurricular activities, and any additional supporting information provided. * Selection Assessments Shortlisted candidates are invited to complete a set of selection assessments designed to evaluate aptitude, analytical skills, and suitability for the specific roles within the graduate programme. * First Round Interviews Successful candidates from the assessment stage are then invited to participate in first\-round interviews. These interviews are typically conducted by the HR team and hiring managers and will focus on exploring your motivations, experiences, and suitability for the programme. * Second\-Round interviews with Senior Management Candidates who successfully navigate the initial interview stage are invited to a second round, where you will have the opportunity to interact with senior management. This stage delves deeper into candidate's potential contributions to the organization and allows senior leaders to assess the alignment of the candidate with the overall strategic goals of the company. * Final Selection After thorough evaluation and consideration of all stages, the final selection is made. Successful candidates will be offered a place on the Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme, marking the beginning of your professional journey with the organisation. HRI is an equal opportunities employer, and we are committed to inclusivity and accessibility. We will provide any necessary reasonable accommodation for candidates during the interview process.

title: Trade Compliance Analyst
description: **Title \- Business Analyst** **Type \- Contract** **Location \- Remote** **Roles and Responsibilities:** * Collaborate with regional trade compliance teams and business stakeholders to gather, document, and refine business requirements and functional processes, adapting them as business needs evolve. * Maintain and oversee day\-to\-day global classification processes to support global import volumes, ensuring accuracy and compliance. * Assist with technology, hardware, and software trade classification, including management of **Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS)** , **Schedule B** , and **Export Control Classification Number (ECCN)** classifications. * Manage compliance activities equally focused on imports and exports (50% each). * Develop and contribute to documented classification justifications based on the **General Rules of Interpretation (GRI)** foundation for global import classifications. * Provide import compliance guidance in adherence to US and international regulations, including conducting research, updating status reports, and coordinating deliverables within the team. * Build, maintain, and analyze data\-informed metrics to generate actionable insights that enhance the global trade compliance program. * Establish and nurture positive business relationships with key stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. * Engage with software development teams to understand and influence evolving compliance technology, identifying opportunities to automate manual processes. * Generate and contribute to performance requirements, timelines, and metrics for all related activities. * Support project management efforts to ensure timely delivery of team assignments and objectives. **Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain, Logistics, or a related field. * Minimum of 5 years of experience as a Business Analyst, preferably within supply chain, logistics, or global trade compliance environments. * Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear documentation including **Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)** and detailed reports. * Proven experience with global trade compliance regulations, including import/export laws and logistics preferred. * Demonstrated ability to independently research and dive deep into complex compliance requests and issues. **Tools and Technologies:** * Proficient in **Smartsheet** , **Power BI** , and **AWS Quicksight** with hands\-on experience creating new dashboards, running data batches, and developing meaningful metrics and reports.

title: Trainee Sheet Metal Worker
company: Ryanair Engineering – Delivered by PAM GmbH
description: All jobs Trainee Sheet Metal Worker Apply now Dublin Airport, Ireland Trainees, Engineering * Facebook * Twitter * LinkedIn * Mail * WhatsApp **Description** Ryanair is Europe’s favourite low fares airline, operating more than 3,700 daily flights from over 95 bases, connecting 250 destinations in over 37 countries and operating a fleet of over 640\+ Aircraft As part of our continuous growth plan, we are seeking to recruit Trainee Sheet Metal Workers for our 12\-week training program with intakes throughout 2026\. The Trainee Sheet Metal Worker programme is open to all. It provides candidates with the opportunity to upskill and pursue a career as a licensed engineer. **Duties Will Include, But Not Limited To** * Responsible for carrying out maintenance tasks as required and allocated with minimal supervision * Maintain a high standard of work in all tasks * Complete the appropriate documentation * Comply with all company procedures, policies, regulations and MOE * Maintain a safe and clean working environment * Ensure the company Health and Safety policy is adhered to * Any other tasks as directed by Supervisor / Manager **Note:** This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the job and its general nature. This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of duties and should not be assumed as such. This job description can be amended and varied from time to time at management’s discretion, without prior notice. **Requirements** * Previous aircraft maintenance experience is desirable, however not essential. * HNC / HND or equivalent qualification in engineering / mechanical is essential. * Background in engineering/maintenance/mechanics is essential. * Exhibit a positive attitude with a willingness to learn. * Excellent spoken and written English is essential. * Must be willing to work shifts (including nights). * Flexible approach to working in a fast\-changing environment. * Successful candidate must be able to provide a clean background check. **Benefits** In addition to offering successful candidates the opportunity to work within the aviation industry, we also offer\- * Structured career growth * Supported learning and training * Competitive Salary * Dynamic work environment * Starting Salary €61\.5k as a Sheet Metal Worker Competencies **Business unit:** Office **Division:** Engineering Office Apply now

title: Training and Implementation Executive
description: At **Alkimii** , we’re on a mission to transform how hospitality businesses manage their people and property operations. Our platform helps hotels simplify operations, improve communication across teams, and run their businesses more efficiently. As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a **Training \& Implementation Executive** to help new customers successfully launch and adopt the Alkimii platform. This is a great opportunity for someone with **who enjoys training, learning new technology and helping teams adopt new systems.** You will initially support implementations alongside experienced team members and progressively take ownership of customer onboarding projects as you grow in the role. **What you’ll be doing** As part of our **Training \& Implementation Team** , you will help guide new customers through their onboarding journey and ensure they feel confident using Alkimii in their day\-to\-day operations. In this role you will: • Support the implementation of the Alkimii platform for new customers across Ireland and the UK • Work closely with customers to understand their operational processes and help configure the system to meet their needs • Deliver training sessions (online and on\-site) to help teams learn how to use the platform effectively • Assist with onboarding activities including preparation, configuration and follow\-up with customers • Provide ongoing support during the early stages of adoption to ensure a smooth transition • Collaborate with the Customer Success and Product teams to share feedback and improve the onboarding experience As you grow in the role, you will gradually take **greater ownership of implementation projects and customer onboarding journeys** . **What success looks like in your first months** • You build a strong understanding of the Alkimii platform and how hospitality teams use it • You confidently support onboarding sessions and training with customers • Customers feel supported and comfortable adopting the system • You contribute ideas to improve the onboarding experience and internal processes **We think you’ll thrive in this role if you:** • Have experience working in hospitality operations, management or eduation. • Enjoy helping people learn and adopt new tools • Are comfortable speaking with customers and delivering presentations • Are organised and able to manage multiple tasks and priorities • Are curious, proactive and eager to develop your career in a growing SaaS company **Requirements** • Strong computer and technical skills • Excellent communication and presentation skills • Fluent written and spoken English • Eligibility to work full\-time in Ireland • Full clean driving licence • Flexibility to travel across Ireland and occasionally to the UK **What can you expect from us:** 💰Full time competitive salary 🩺 Wellbeing programs, learning and growth opportunities 🧘 ♀️ Flexible working week, because we take seriously the productivity and wellbeing of our team 🏝️ 25 days vacation 🚗 Mileage and expenses 🚀 You will work on meaningful and challenging tasks in a relaxed yet supportive environment. 🙋 4,000 Employee Referral Program 🌅 Work in a beautiful building located on Skerries Harbour overlooking the pier! we get the best sunsets! 👯 Quarterly team meet\-ups \& annual gathering 💲Clevercards \- Employee Benefits **Why join Alkimii?** We’re a team that cares deeply about our customers and about each other. Our culture is built on kindness, curiosity and ownership, and we’re always looking for ways to improve how we work and how we support hospitality teams around the world. If you enjoy solving problems, helping people and being part of a growing company where your ideas matter, we’d love to hear from you. *Before you apply, we feel it’s important that you know a bit more about us…* We: * Enjoy being up early enough to see the sun rise * Appreciate a good barista coffee * Discipline ourselves with morning workouts * Feed our curiosity with a good book * Don’t take ourselves too seriously * Constantly ask ourselves how we can make it easier for our customers * Love long walks on the beach (yes, really!) All sounding good? Check out what your new commute might look like: https://goo.gl/maps/cf5LPcJmCBBPYZFR8

title: Underwriter
description: **Underwriter – Private Motor Insurance** **Location:** IFSC, Dublin City / Hybrid **Reporting to:** Senior Underwriter / Head of Underwriting Operations **Type:** Full\-time, permanent **About the role** Fastnet Underwriting Limited is an award winning MGA offering private motor insurance in Ireland through our broker and direct network. We design and distribute non\-standard products underwritten by AIG. We operate using an advanced digital platform to deliver our product. This includes Risk Handler to facilitate smooth customer on\-boarding and broker communication, along with a data enrichment program from LexisNexis. With a strong and experienced team, we pride ourselves on delivering the highest level of service to brokers. We're looking for an Underwriter to join our private motor insurance team. This is an ideal opportunity for someone currently working in a brokerage who understands the customer and intermediary side of the business and now wants to move into underwriting and learn the discipline from the inside. You'll work closely with experienced underwriters, applying our underwriting rules and pricing to new business, renewals, and mid\-term adjustments. Over time you'll take on greater authority and ownership as your judgement develops. We'll support you with structured training, mentoring, and the qualifications you need to build a long\-term career in underwriting. No prior underwriting experience is required — what matters is a solid grounding in motor insurance, good judgement, and a genuine appetite to learn. **What you'll do** * Assess and process new business, renewals, and mid\-term adjustments within agreed underwriting guidelines and authority limits. * Support our underwriting desk and liase with our network of brokers, handle referrals and queries from brokers and intermediaries in a professional and timely way * Manage and maintain our direct offerings through various schemes and comparison sites * Review risk information, apply rating and underwriting rules, and refer cases that fall outside guidelines to senior colleagues. * Ensure policies are underwritten accurately and in line with the delegated authority agreement and regulatory requirements. * Maintain accurate records and support data quality across the book. * Contribute to the continuous review of underwriting rules, pricing, and processes, flagging trends or anomalies you spot. * Build strong working relationships with brokers, colleagues, and capacity partners. **What you'll bring** * APA (Accredited Product Adviser) or working towards CIP (Certified Insurance Practitioner), or equivalent. * Experience in motor insurance, ideally gained in a broker or intermediary environment and ideally a good working knowledge of the Applied Relay 360 system. * A good understanding of motor cover, policy types, and the quote\-to\-bind process. * Strong attention to detail and comfort working with numbers and rules. * Sound judgement and the confidence to ask questions and escalate appropriately. * Clear written and verbal communication. * Excellent customer service skills and telephone manner * Proficient in all Microsoft Office Suites * A genuine interest in underwriting as a career path. **What we offer** * Structured training and mentoring from experienced underwriters. * Support towards professional qualifications (APA / CIP), including study time and exam costs. * A clear development path from junior to fully\-fledged underwriter. * Competitive salary, pension, hybrid working, and other benefits * A collaborative team where you'll see the whole underwriting cycle, not just one corner of it. **How to apply** Send your CV and a short note on why you'd like to move into underwriting to careers@fastnetunderwriting.com. We're happy to have an informal chat first if you'd like to learn more about the role before applying. *Fastnet Underwriting Limited is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all backgrounds.*

title: Vending Machine Merchandiser & Delivery Driver (Dublin)
description: BDS Vending Solutions, part of Coca\-Cola HBC group, is expanding and looking for reliable, customer\-focused drivers to join our Dublin team! This role combines Delivery Driving \& Vending Machine Merchandising, giving you a varied workday across customer sites nationwide. We provide full training, set shift pattern, and the opportunity to build a long\-term career with a growing company. **Available Shifts:** \- Monday to Friday 6:30am\-3pm Overtime opportunities are also available! **Work Location:** On the road **Head Office Location:** Parkwest, Dublin 12 **Pay:** Starting hourly salary **€15:49 with a guaranteed increase to €15\.95 after 6mths** (upon completion of probation). **Job Type:** Full\-time \| Permanent **What You’ll Do** * Deliver stock to customer locations and team members across Ireland * Restock, clean, merchandise, and rotate products in vending machines * Maintain equipment and ensure machines are operating correctly * Provide excellent customer service at every site * Report machine issues or stock shortages to our support team * Maintain a professional appearance and safe working practices **What We’re Looking For** * Full, clean B Manual Driving Licence (required) * A customer\-focused attitude * Comfortable driving a Van and lifting shallow crates of snacks and drinks (weighting max 17kg) * Good conversational English and communication skills * Strong time management and reliability * Motivation to work independently and deliver excellent results No prior vending or delivery experience? No problem. Full training will be provided. **What we Offer** * Fulltime hours * Regular set shift * Permanent position with job security * 20 days annual leave * Full uniform and PPE provided * Comprehensive training * Employee Assistance Programme * Opportunities for overtime and career development * A supportive and inclusive workplace culture Pay: €32,219\.20\-€36,246\.60 per year Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * Free or subsidised travel * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Warehouse Operative (Days) - Naas
description: At Aldi, every day is different. As a Operative, you play a key role in keeping our operations running smoothly. Using a headset to receive instructions, you’ll pick products accurately, check paperwork, and maintain safety while working efficiently in a high\-energy environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Select products accurately and at pace to meet picking targets * Follow instructions carefully and double\-check paperwork for accuracy * Operate Mechanical Handling Equipment (MHE) safely and efficiently (training provided) * Opportunities in our Ambient, Chiller and Freezer sections **Skills \& Experience** * Hardworking and ready for a physical, fast paced role * Reliable and flexible with a can\-do attitude in a fast\-moving environment * Strong attention to detail, especially when picking stock **Our Benefits** * Competitive pay with pay rises linked to your time with Aldi * There is an additional rate of €1\.00 per hour for any hours worked in the freezer. * 30\+ hours per week (minimum contract hours are 30 and can rise to an average of 36\) * 4 weeks paid annual leave plus bank holidays * Comprehensive training and ongoing development to build your career * Access to wellbeing programmes and lifestyle discounts * Family leave including maternity, paternity, neonatal and adoption leave after one year * Shift times vary, however, we will always try to make sure you typically work 7\-8 hours.

title: Yard Operative - Skip Hire Waste Sorter - Permanent, Full-Time - Ardee, Co. Louth
description: Express Skip Hire (part of Dunard Recycling Ltd) is hiring waste sorters / yard operatives to join our recycling team at our base outside Ardee. The job is sorting the construction and demolition waste that comes in from our skips — separating it by hand into timber, green waste, general rubbish and other materials. It's hands\-on, physical work in the yard, and it's the heart of what we do. **The pay is excellent for the right candidates,**— we pay strongly for experience and reliability, and we look after the people who turn up and do it right. For the right person, there's steady, well\-paid work here week in, week out. **What we offer:** * Strong rates of pay, with more for experience * Permanent, full\-time, secure work with a local family company * Company pension option * Immediate start available **What we're looking for:** * A strong work ethic * Good spoken English, for safety and working with the team * Previous experience in waste sorting or recycling is a real advantage * Reliable and physically fit for active, hands\-on work **To apply:** Call the office, 8:00am–5:30pm: 041 683 9684 Or email your CV to: HR@DunardRecycling.ie Or Whats App message to: 087 346 6881 We look forward to hearing from you. Pay: From €35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person
